question_types.type: Determine instantaneous velocity from position-time slope
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the derivative relationship between position and velocity on a graph.

question_types.type: Calculate displacement from velocity-time area
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Requires integral analysis of velocity data over time intervals.

question_types.type: Determine instantaneous acceleration from velocity-time slope
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the rate of change of velocity at specific time points.

question_types.type: Solve for linear motion variables using kinematic equations
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Uses algebraic routines to solve for unknown motion variables under constant acceleration.

question_types.type: Apply constant gravitational acceleration to vertical free-fall
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Specifically deals with gravity as a constant acceleration downward near Earth.

question_types.type: Analyze vectors as resultants of perpendicular components
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the mathematical model of vector addition and resultants.

question_types.type: Resolve vectors into perpendicular components using trigonometry
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Requires specific trig functions to decouple vector directions.

question_types.type: Separate 2D motion into independent components for analysis
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: The procedural core is analyzing x and y dimensions separately for the same object.

question_types.type: Predict flight time and range for horizontal projectiles
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Specific subclass of 2D motion with zero initial vertical velocity.

question_types.type: Predict target clearance for projectiles launched at angles
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Requires finding vertical position at a specific horizontal displacement.

question_types.type: Convert motion measurements between different inertial reference frames
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the transformation of quantities based on the choice of observer.

question_types.type: Combine motion vectors to find relative velocity in 1D
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Specifically uses vector addition/subtraction to calculate observed speed.

question_types.type: Identify reference frame effects on observed motion direction
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on directional changes in displacement and velocity due to observer frame choice.

question_types.type: Represent motion qualitatively through narrative descriptions
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Translation task from physical scenarios to coherent verbal explanations.

question_types.type: Create motion diagrams to represent physical situations
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on discrete visual representations of objects at regular time intervals.

question_types.type: Distinguish between distance and displacement in complex paths
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Tests the fundamental scalar-versus-vector nature of position changes.

question_types.type: Calculate average velocity using displacement and time intervals
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the state-based ratio definition of velocity.

question_types.type: Calculate average acceleration from velocity change over time
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the state-based ratio definition of acceleration.

question_types.type: Linearize kinematics data sets to determine physical constants
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Lab-centric task of transforming non-linear data into straight-line graphs to extract slope meaning.

question_types.type: Design an experiment to verify uniform acceleration
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Requires choosing representations and measurements that can reveal whether acceleration stays constant.

question_types.type: Locate center of mass for symmetrical mass distributions
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on identifying lines of symmetry to find a system's balance point.

question_types.type: Model a system as a single point at the center of mass
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Procedural shift where internal structure is ignored and the system is treated as a single object at its center of mass.

question_types.type: Draw and label free-body diagrams for interacting objects
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: The essential representation task for identifying all forces exerted on the chosen object by the environment.

question_types.type: Construct Newton's Third Law force pairs for interacting bodies
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on identifying equal and opposite forces on different interacting objects.

question_types.type: Analyze net force for systems in translational equilibrium
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Specifically applies translational equilibrium where the vector sum of forces is zero.

question_types.type: Solve for object acceleration using net force and mass
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Standard numerical and symbolic application of Newton's Second Law.

question_types.type: Determine tension magnitudes in connected systems
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Analyzes how force is transmitted through ideal or nonideal strings in connected systems.

question_types.type: Resolve weight into perpendicular components on inclined planes
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Procedurally distinct due to the tilted coordinate system used to translate an incline diagram into equations.

question_types.type: Analyze normal force variation in accelerating elevators
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Addresses the misconception that normal force must equal weight even when the system accelerates vertically.

question_types.type: Differentiate between static and kinetic friction thresholds
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Tests the transition from static friction adjusting to prevent slipping to kinetic friction during sliding.

question_types.type: Calculate kinetic friction using normal force and coefficient
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the constant dissipative force during sliding motion.

question_types.type: Predict acceleration changes when net force is doubled
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Proportional reasoning task for scaling force and mass parameters under Newton's Second Law.

question_types.type: Determine gravitational force between two massive objects
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Specifically uses Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation to relate force, mass, and separation.

question_types.type: Apply the equivalence of gravitational and inertial mass
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: A conceptual bridge testing why inertial mass and gravitational mass are treated as experimentally equivalent.

question_types.type: Predict gravitational field strength scaling with planet radius
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Scaling task based on gravitational field strength as a position-dependent noncontact interaction.

question_types.type: Calculate restoring force in springs using displacement
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Specifically applies Hooke's Law for position-dependent forces.

question_types.type: Determine centripetal acceleration magnitude for circular motion
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Focuses on the inward acceleration required for circular motion and its v^2/r magnitude relationship.

question_types.type: Analyze centripetal force requirements to maintain circular paths
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Requires matching real external forces such as tension or gravity to the centripetal acceleration requirement.

question_types.type: Design an experiment to measure kinetic friction coefficient
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Lab design task focused on measuring the kinetic-friction relationship through force and motion data.

question_types.type: Translate free-body diagrams into systems of equations
question_types.qt_distinctness_reason: Tests the specific bridge between a free-body diagram, a convenient coordinate system, and the resulting algebraic equations.
